name: My Bouillabaisse
recipeInstructions:
1. Skin the fish and cut into serving-sized pieces. Use fillets. Flounder or other firm white fish may be substituted.
2. Reserve broth from steamed clams for use in the bouillabaisse.
3. Heat olive oil in a large pot and cook garlic and onion until soft over medium heat.
4. Add leek and cook for 10 minutes.
5. Add tomatoes and sugar; simmer for 5 minutes.
6. Add remaining ingredients except seafood, then simmer covered for 30 minutes.
7. Strain the broth, discarding orange zest and bay leaf. Puree some of the vegetables with part of the broth and return to the pot.
8. Adjust salt and pepper to taste. Refrigerate or freeze this portion if desired.
9. Strain broth from steamed clams and add to the pot.
10. Add clams (diced if preferred) along with remaining seafood and dark leek portion.
11. Simmer for 3-5 minutes, correcting seasonings as needed. Serve in bowls with garlic bread and Sauce Rouille.
